claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child (or children) you're claiming for.**

**How old is the child?**

Please let me know which category applies:
- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you have multiple children you'd like to claim for, please tell me their names and ages, and we can assess each one.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, yes I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have one child, Alex, who's 13 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, **Alex**, who is **13 years old**. Since Alex is under 16, that's a good start for eligibility.

Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about Alex:**

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

This includes any paid employment Alex might have. At 13, most children aren't in significant paid work, but I need to confirm this.

- **Yes** - Alex works less than 24 hours per week or doesn't have paid work
- **No** - Alex works 24 hours or more per week

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't have any paid work at all.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, so Alex doesn't have any paid work. That means the answer is **yes** - Alex works less than 24 hours per week (in this case, zero hours).

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about Alex:**

**Is Alex NOT receiving any of the following benefits in their own right?**
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit

In other words, is Alex free from receiving these benefits personally?

- **Yes** - Alex is not receiving ESA or Universal Credit in their own name
- **No** - Alex is receiving one of these benefits

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. Alex doesn't receive any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about Alex:**

**Is Alex currently in an apprenticeship?**

Most apprenticeships disqualify a child from Child Benefit eligibility, with some exceptions. Here are the details:

- **Disqualifying apprenticeships:**
  - All apprenticeships in England (Intermediate, Advanced, Higher, Degree)
  - Modern Apprenticeships in Scotland
  - Apprenticeships in Wales (except Foundation level)

- **Approved apprenticeships (still eligible for Child Benefit):**
  - Foundation Apprenticeships in Wales

At 13, Alex is likely not in an apprenticeship, but please confirm:

- **Not in any apprenticeship**
- **In an approved apprenticeship** (e.g., Welsh Foundation Apprenticeship)
- **In a disqualifying apprenticeship** (any other type listed above)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in an apprenticeship.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Alex is not in an apprenticeship.

**Now I need to check your residency status.**

**Do you meet UK residency requirements?**

You qualify if **any one** of the following applies to you:

1. **Normally lives in the UK** - You usually live in the UK
2. **Settled status** - You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Pre-settled status and working** - You have pre-settled status and are working/earning above the National Insurance threshold
4. **Pre-settled status and seeking work** - You have pre-settled status and are actively looking for work (can claim for up to 91 days)
5. **Pre-settled status with sufficient resources** - You have pre-settled status and can support yourself financially (not claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit)
6. **Pre-settled status and studying** - You have pre-settled status and are studying with sufficient financial resources
7. **Pre-settled status as family member** - You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. **Crown servant abroad** - You're living abroad as a Crown servant

**Do any of these apply to you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Just to clarify a bit further:

**Are you living abroad as a Crown servant?**

Crown servants include people such as:
- Members of the armed forces stationed overseas
- Diplomats or embassy staff
- Civil servants posted abroad by the UK government

If you're working for the UK government abroad, you may still qualify for Child Benefit.

- **Yes** - I am a Crown servant living abroad
- **No** - I am living abroad but not as a Crown servant

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant. I'm just living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Unfortunately, since you're living abroad and are not a Crown servant, you don't meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'm sorry to share this news, but based on the information you've provided, I need to report the outcome of the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## Assessment Result: **NOT ELIGIBLE**

### Summary for Alex

| Child | Age | Eligible? |
|-------|-----|-----------|
| Alex | 13 | ❌ No |

### Reason

You **do not meet the UK residency requirements** for Child Benefit. To claim Child Benefit, you must:

- Normally live in the UK, **or**
- Have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with certain conditions), **or**
- Be a Crown servant living abroad

Since you're living abroad and are not a Crown servant, you unfortunately don't qualify at this time.

### What You Can Do

- **If your situation changes** and you return to live in the UK, you may become eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex at that point.
- **If you work for the UK government abroad** in the future, you may qualify as a Crown servant.
- You may also want to check if the country you're living in has its own family benefit schemes you could apply for.

I'm sorry I couldn't deliver better news. If you have any questions or your circumstances change, please feel free to reach out again.

reasonings: Alex is 13 years old (under 16), has no paid work, doesn't receive benefits in their own right, and is not in an apprenticeship - all of which would normally support eligibility. However, the claimant (Alex's parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad and is not a Crown servant, and does not qualify under any of the other residency paths (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, etc.). Therefore, the claimant is not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit because they do not meet the UK residency requirements. While Alex meets all the child-related criteria (under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, not in an apprenticeship), the claimant must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances to claim Child Benefit. Since the claimant lives abroad and is not a Crown servant, they do not qualify.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.
